basedOn: Based on root font-size: {rootFontSize}px · Precision: {precision} decimals

descriptions.aeoDefinition: CSS Unit Converter is a free online tool for converting between px, rem, em, vw, vh, vmin, and vmax. Batch convert CSS code with customizable root font size and viewport dimensions. Runs entirely in your browser.
descriptions.whatIsTitle: What is the CSS Unit Converter?
descriptions.whatIsP1: The CSS Unit Converter lets you instantly convert between CSS length units — px, rem, em, vw, vh, vmin, and vmax. Set your root font size, parent font size, and viewport dimensions for accurate results.
descriptions.whatIsP2: Paste CSS code for batch conversion, or use the reference table for common px-to-rem and px-to-vw mappings.
descriptions.step1Text: Type a numeric value and select the source unit. All equivalent values are calculated in real time.
descriptions.step2Text: Paste your CSS code, choose a conversion direction, and get the converted output with matched values highlighted.
descriptions.step3Text: Use the reference tab for quick lookups of common px-to-rem, px-to-em, and px-to-vw conversions.
descriptions.faq1Q: When should I use rem vs em?
descriptions.faq1A: Use rem for consistent sizing relative to the root font size — ideal for margins, padding, and layout. Use em for typography that should scale with its parent element, like nested font sizes in components.
descriptions.faq2Q: How do viewport units (vw, vh) work?
descriptions.faq2A: 1vw equals 1% of the viewport width, and 1vh equals 1% of the viewport height. They are useful for full-width or full-height layouts that adapt to the browser window size.
descriptions.faq3A: Percentages depend on the containing element and the specific CSS property, so there is no universal conversion factor. This tool only supports units with fixed, predictable conversion bases.
